key_points: Athlete housing described as 'really comfortable setup' by observers
No specific complaints about regular student housing surfaced
Limited direct student data on non-athlete dorm quality
key_quotes: That dorm situation looks like a really comfortable setup. Take advantage of it as long as necessary.
Comparison is the thief of all joy.

key_points: Strong Greek life presence; sororities hold secret ritual practices
Big college-town atmosphere with heavy football culture dominance
Norman described as 'nice small town outside OKC' — not a big city
key_quotes: It's a neat culture if you want to be immersed in being a Sooner.
Greeks have tons of secret stuff they do. It's her secret club doing secret stuff.
Norman is... meh.

key_points: D1 athlete dining is exceptional — dedicated nutrition staff and quality food
Regular students on a budget can get by on ~$25/week for food
No major complaints about dining halls from non-athletes in the data
key_quotes: I love that there is just good ass food.
Every time a D1 Football player leaves a building he has someone waiting with boxes full of fancy drinks, food and snacks.
Most people order in pizza or Chinese or something and that's like under $20.

key_points: One student described leaving OU due to mental health struggles before returning
No mentions of dedicated mental health resources or counseling access
Injury and academic pressure documented as real stressors for athletes
key_quotes: I left because of mental health... I'm so glad that your mental health is in a better place now.
Gets tough mentally, it's a dark place being injured.
Spend an extra few years at school or spend a 40 year career wishing you had done something else.

key_points: National Merit Scholars get full-ride plus stipends — one of the best deals in the country
Oklahoma Promise covers full tuition for qualifying low-income in-state students
International students can get near-full scholarships, making OU a rare affordable option
key_quotes: OU used to let you use your scholarship for your first year of grad school, including law and medicine. Incredibly valuable benefit.
Got into my safety school! University of Oklahoma hit me up with that sweet sweet scholarship money once Nat'l Merit Finalists come out.
I'm a low income oklahoma resident so I qualified for Oklahoma promise which automatically gives full tuition.

key_points: Meteorology/atmospheric science program is legitimately top-tier and nationally recognized
Engineering and CS programs are solid but not elite nationally
Honors college adds research access and program quality for high-achieving students
key_quotes: OU's med school ranking is high in primary care.
They have a great meteorology program and Norman is home to all kinds of important weather stuff.
UCSB isn't worth the extra 160K over 4 years, especially when you're in the honors college and the scholars program at OU.

key_points: Serious recent scandal raised concerns about academic integrity and political interference
International application responses took 11+ weeks with no reply to emails
Admissions portal confusingly shows enrollment deposit before official acceptance letter arrives
key_quotes: OU degrees are under such fire rn. Like actually being blacklisted by employers. It will take a long time for them to recover from such a scandal.
A situation just unfolded at OU that raises serious questions about academic integrity, administrative independence, and how the institution responds when political pressure mounts.
I've already contacted them, but no answer.

key_points: Beautiful campus — widely praised by students, alumni, and visitors alike
Norman is a classic college town but isolated; OKC is nearby but not walkable
Weather is Oklahoma-typical: hot summers, tornado risk, mild winters
key_quotes: Nice small town outside OKC. Beautiful campus. It's big college town but enough students there for other interests.
Norman is... meh.
As much time as I've spent on campus at OU I've never seen the athlete's perspective so it's always dope to get a glimpse!

key_points: Recent administrative scandal actively flagged as causing employer blacklisting of OU degrees
Energy management program feeds directly into high-paying oil/gas industry jobs
Strong alumni network and regional brand — but limited national employer reach
key_quotes: OU degrees are under such fire rn. Like actually being blacklisted by employers.
Energy management... I heard they had a good program and I'd be making money out of school.
Just go to OU and do well. Outcomes can be the same.

key_points: For National Merit or Oklahoma Promise students, OU is one of the best deals in the US
Out-of-state without merit aid is ~$36k/year — hard to justify over in-state options
Full-ride plus stipend beats many prestigious schools in pure financial terms
key_quotes: Berkeley is good, but not $300K+ good.
I got into the honors college and the pre law scholars program at OU... UCSB isn't worth the extra 160K.
OU used to let you use your scholarship for your first year of grad school, including law and medicine. Incredibly valuable benefit.

key_points: Extremely strong football and sports culture — this dominates campus identity
Friendly, welcoming atmosphere with heavy Christian faith presence throughout
Classic Big 12 state school energy: spirited, community-focused, conservative-leaning
key_quotes: It's a neat culture if you want to be immersed in being a Sooner. I know people who really love the town and the atmosphere.
LGBT wise I'd say probably none of the three are 'great'... all three lean right.
Nice music and a video for the accepted students.

key_points: **Academic Integrity**: Recent scandal flagged as causing employer blacklisting of OU degrees
**Administration**: Political interference in university operations raised by multiple students publicly
**Communication**: International applicants report weeks of silence and unanswered emails from admissions
key_quotes: OU degrees are under such fire rn. Like actually being blacklisted by employers. It will take a long time for them to recover.
A situation just unfolded at OU that raises serious questions about academic integrity, administrative independence, and how the institution responds when political pressure mounts.
Please remain cognisant of this when you choose to spend four years of your life there.

key_points: Meteorology program is genuinely elite — Norman hosts NOAA and major weather research centers
National Merit full-ride includes grad school first year — almost no other school offers this
D1 athlete facilities are described as world-class even by former players from decades ago
key_quotes: OU used to let you use your scholarship for your first year of grad school, including law and medicine. Incredibly valuable benefit.
Norman is home to all kinds of important weather stuff.
Every time I see the facilities I'm in awe. First time I saw them was on my official visit… in 1992. Things have changed just a bit.
